signals.headline: Institutional Demand Surge Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Institutional Demand Surge Confirmed | YES | HIGH confidence**

Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded **$471 million in net inflows on April 6**, the strongest single day since late February, with BlackRock's IBIT, Fidelity's FBTC, and Ark Invest's ARKB absorbing the bulk[2]. Morgan Stanley's MSBT launch added **$34 million in day-one inflows**, ranking in the top 1% of all ETF launches historically[2].

signals.headline: Bitcoin Breaks $76,000 Resistance Level | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Breaks $76,000 Resistance Level | YES | HIGH confidence**

Bitcoin is **testing $76,000 resistance** on the 4-hour timeframe with bulls pressing hard against this level[4]. If breached, this would open the door to the top of a 12-week bear flag, representing a significant technical breakout[4].

signals.headline: Macro Catalyst: Iran Ceasefire Impact | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Macro Catalyst: Iran Ceasefire Impact | YES | HIGH confidence**

A U.S.-Iran ceasefire collapsed crude oil prices from $112 per barrel, triggering **$427 million in crypto short liquidations** in 48 hours—the largest since late February[2]. This repriced Fed rate cut expectations from zero to possible in the second half of 2026[2].
